title: Email Multiple Targets/Columns

posts.author: Kasper (Jesse Churchill)
posts.cookedHtml:
The issue here is when I need to send the EXACT same email to multiple targets.
I want a feature that lets me select multiple targets not just one column or one team so:

Send Email to “XYZ people columns or multiple email columns”

My specific use case:
To automate emails I use “people columns” as my target variable, so I don’t have to keep changing multiple automations every time staff change.

  1. First I automate the assignment of people into “XYZ people columns” based on their region, such as Admin, Directors, and Tech Team.
  2. Then, I go to automations/integrations, select my trigger, and then action: send email to “X person column”
  3. Then, add another action send email to “Y person column”
  4. Then, “Z person column”.

When the email is NOT exactly the same this not an issue. But often it’s a simple email I need sent to 3 different teams like: “This New Employee has began onboarding, please begin your next steps”. So it’s very redundant to create 3 actions that look exactly the same except for the email target.

posts.author: noam.lapidot (Noam Lapidot)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Kasper a possible workaround is using SuperMail app which allows also cc’ing people.
With that app, you can also send emails to a text column and have there multiple email addresses sepreated by ;
